back: UX Design is the process of designing digital products that are user-friendly, efficient, and enjoyable. It focuses on understanding user needs and creating meaningful experiences.

Visibility of system status, Match between system and real world, User control and freedom, Consistency and standards, Error prevention, Recognition rather than recall, Flexibility and efficiency of use, Aesthetic and minimalist design, Help users recognize errors, Help and documentation.

UCD is an iterative design approach that places users at the center of all design decisions. It involves understanding user needs, involving users in testing, and iteratively improving designs based on feedback.
IA is the structural design of information content. It involves organizing, structuring, and labeling content effectively to help users find information and complete tasks.
A wireframe is a basic visual guide that shows the skeletal structure of a website or app. It displays layout, content placement, and basic UI elements without styling or graphics.
A prototype is an interactive model of a product that simulates the final user experience. It allows testing and validation of design decisions before full development.

Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) are international standards for making web content accessible to people with disabilities. It covers perceivability, operability, understandability, and robustness.
Fitts's Law states that the time to acquire a target is a function of the distance to and size of the target. Larger, closer targets are easier to click.
Hicks's Law states that the time it takes to make a decision increases with the number and complexity of choices. Fewer options lead to faster decision-making.
Design principles describing how humans perceive visual elements: Proximity, Similarity, Continuity, Closure, Figure-Ground, and Common Region.
Cognitive load refers to the mental effort required to process information. Good UX design minimizes unnecessary cognitive load through clear layout and simple navigation.
A user persona is a fictional character representing a user type. It is created from user research to help designers understand user goals, needs, and behaviors.
A/B testing is a method comparing two versions of a webpage or app to determine which performs better. Users are randomly assigned to either version for comparison.
Visual hierarchy is the arrangement of elements to show their order of importance. It guides users' attention using size, color, contrast, spacing, and positioning.
Usability testing is evaluating a product by testing it with representative users who attempt to complete typical tasks. Researchers observe and collect feedback to identify usability issues.
